description: You are a copy editor for a history PhD student. Open the draft chapter at /home/ga/Documents/Erie_Canal_Draft.odt and replace the five citation placeholders ([1], [2], etc.) with proper footnotes using the citation text provided in /home/ga/Documents/citation_source.json. Format the Bibliography section by applying a 0.5" hanging indent to the entries. Apply the 'Heading 1' style to the chapter title and the 'Bibliography' header. Add centered page numbers to the footer. Save the final document as /home/ga/Documents/Chapter_2_Final.odt.

citations.1: Peter L. Bernstein, Wedding of the Waters: The Erie Canal and the Making of a Great Nation (New York: W.W. Norton, 2005), 45.
citations.2: Carol Sheriff, The Artificial River: The Erie Canal and the Paradox of Progress (New York: Hill and Wang, 1996), 12.
citations.3: Ronald E. Shaw, Erie Water West: A History of the Erie Canal (Lexington: University of Kentucky Press, 1966), 88.
citations.4: Robert G. Albion, The Rise of New York Port (New York: Scribner, 1939), 202.
